name: Muhammad ibn Ashath
title: Deceitful Commander
shortDescription: A commander of Ibn Ziyad's army who arrested Muslim ibn Aqeel and arrogantly insulted the Imam in Karbala.
fullStory:
Muhammad ibn al-Ash'ath ibn Qays al-Kindi was a senior commander of Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad in Kufa. His family was known for hypocrisy and enmity towards the Ahl al-Bayt.

He was the one who, in Kufa, deceived and arrested Muslim ibn Aqeel by giving him a false promise of safe conduct, and then broke his promise. In the event of Ashura, Ibn Ziyad sent him to Karbala with a massive army.

On Ashura, he reached the height of arrogance. When Imam Hussain (AS) asked: 'Am I not the son of your Prophet's daughter?' Muhammad ibn al-Ash'ath insolently shouted: 'We don't know what you are saying!'

Also, when the Imam was praying, Ibn al-Ash'ath insulted him, and the Imam cursed him. Immediately after the Imam's curse, a scorpion (or tarantula) stung him, and he fell from his horse in pain and disgrace and perished (some narrations place his death during Mukhtar's uprising, but the curse incident is very famous).
quoteArabic: يا رَبِّ خُذْهُ بَعْدَ اليَوْمِ أَخْذَةَ عَزيزٍ مُقْتَدِرٍ...
quote: (Imam's curse upon him:) O Lord! Seize him with your might and power...
